scripts.dev: trap 'kill 0' EXIT; tsup --watch --no-dts & vite --config vite.docs.config.ts & wait

principles: Simplicity above all; abstractions are extracted from proven duplication, never predicted.
Solve the stated problem, not adjacent ones — a bug fix is not a refactoring opportunity.
Dependencies flow inward: shared packages never depend on application code.
Let the type system carry its weight; if a type is hard to express, rethink the design.
packages/ui/src/recipes/ has a public substrate (ryu/) and an internal layer (kata/, waku/); the internal/public boundary is pinned by src/__tests__/recipes/internal-boundary.test.ts.

Files must be self-identifying out of context: components use <name>.tsx / <name>-<part>.tsx; hooks use use-<name>-<hook>.ts; every component or hook file exports a symbol whose PascalCase form matches its filename (enforced by packages/ui/src/__tests__/components/boundary/component-filename-boundary.test.ts).
Never extend the inline ALLOWLIST in component-filename-boundary.test.ts for new files — fix the file or fix the export instead.
Imperative-mood, atomic commits; each commit represents one logical change.
Inside the editing loop, never run the full ui test suite (`pnpm --filter ui test` or `pnpm test`); use scoped commands and let the lefthook pre-commit gate catch the rest.
When a check fails after an edit, fix the cause; never weaken the check (no deleting assertions, .skip, or stripping expect calls to make a suite green).
Work is done only when /postmortem returns PROCEED and the diff has been self-reviewed.

vocabularyGlossary.ryu: Public substrate layer under packages/ui/src/recipes/ — cross-cutting scales and currents re-exported from src/recipes/index.ts.
vocabularyGlossary.kata: Internal per-component recipe layer under packages/ui/src/recipes/kata/ — imported via relative paths, deliberately not in package.json exports.
vocabularyGlossary.waku: Internal control primitive layer under packages/ui/src/recipes/waku/ — same internal-only boundary as kata.
vocabularyGlossary.Manifest: Canonical project profile at ./manifest.json produced by /repo:manifest and consumed by every other skill.
vocabularyGlossary.Brief: Output of /deliberate — a precise problem statement synthesized from ABCD-style answers to dimension gaps.

mandatorySkills.trigger: ./manifest.json is missing or invalidated (every other skill stops with this instruction when absent)
request is a fuzzy idea from which no concrete proposal can be formed
before declaring work done — must return PROCEED, or every chained downstream skill must return PASS
scaffolding a new UI component in packages/ui — canonical source for [layout-heuristics] and [framework-discipline]
writing tests for components, hooks, utilities, or modules
only via /postmortem (diff mode) or /ui:component:compose & /tests:compose (file mode) — do not invoke directly outside those paths unless explicitly asked

notes: apps/tms, packages/heimdall, and packages/sindri exist on disk as residual directories without package.json (only .turbo/ and dist/ artifacts). Git log shows tms was removed and heimdall/sindri were renamed to auth/shared — these directories should be cleaned up.
Both .husky/ and lefthook.yml are present; lefthook is the active tool (root package.json `prepare` script runs `lefthook install`).
No CI provider detected — no .github/workflows, .gitlab-ci.yml, or .circleci/config.yml.
packages/ui is the only package with a test runner (vitest), matching the documented testing policy in CLAUDE.md.

packages/auth and packages/shared list `next` in devDependencies/peerDependencies and so resolve to framework=next per the discovery table, though they ship as libraries (tsup build) consumed by the Next apps.
